_notes: Lee Example 2.1 (ideal pressure buildup): inputs, straight-line anchors and answers are printed in the source; document verified 2026-07-18 (course reproduction of the textbook example, home.czu.cz/storage/575/52485_2010-Ch-9-Chapter-2-Pressure-Buildup-Test.pdf).

The book presents the buildup data as the Horner straight line (slope 100 psi/cycle, pws = 1764 psig at dt = 1 hr i.e. Horner ratio 73). Points below are reconstructed exactly from that printed line; consistency check: the line gives 1950.3 psig at unit Horner ratio, matching the printed pi = 1950 psig.
Pressures are psig throughout (the skin and slope arithmetic uses differences, so gauge units are consistent).

Earlougher buildup (second fixture, closes the WT2 hard gate): Ahmed, Reservoir Engineering Handbook, 4th ed. (Elsevier, 2010), Example 6-26 / Table 6-5, itself reproducing Earlougher, Advances in Well Test Analysis, SPE Monograph 5 (1977). Full 31-row published table and given data typed verbatim from the book PDF on 2026-07-18 (irmat-ucan.com library copy, pp. 473-475). Printed answers: m = 40 psi/cycle, k = 12.8 md, p1hr = 3266 psig, s = 8.6, dp_skin = 299 psi.

Known typo in the printed Step 3 arithmetic: it shows 0.22 for viscosity while the given data say mu_o = 0.20 cp; 0.20 reproduces the printed k = 12.8 md exactly (0.22 would give 14.1), so 0.20 is used here.

The book's answers come from a graphically drawn straight line. The harness regresses over the mid-time region instead: mtr.dtMin = 1.05 hr per the book's own storage rule (log-log unit slope ends well before 1 hr; afterflow ceases near dt = 1 hr) with all later points on Earlougher's line. Regression gives m = 39.4, k = 13.0, p1hr = 3265.7, s = 8.79; tolerances below cover that graphical-vs-regression gap.
p* is not a printed answer in Example 6-26, so pStar is not checked for this fixture.

buildups.citation: Lee (1982) Well Testing, SPE Textbook Vol. 1, Example 2.1 (ideal buildup)
Ahmed, Reservoir Engineering Handbook 4th ed., Ex. 6-26 / Table 6-5 (Earlougher 1977 buildup)
